titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 596

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=908 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:22:01.907332+00 | translation moa=909 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:22:01.920288+00

div.p:
Will’ . Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . Clericis et laicis salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri me concessisse et hac carta mea
confirmasse deo et sancto cuthberto . et Monachis eius de Dunelmo donationem Gospatrici comitis . et successorum eius . scilicet Nesebite . et
uillam . de Ederham . et ęcclesiam eiusdem uille . cum omnibus capellis et pertinentiis suis . in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam tenendam ita
libere . et quiete plenarie et honorifice  sicut carte comitis Gospatr’ et successorum eius testantur . et confirmant . Testibus . Ricardo Episcopo sancti Andree .
Nicholao Cancellario . Ricardo capellano . Ricardo de Moreuilla consabulario . Wellmo filio Alani dapifero . Dauid . olifard . Dolfino capellano . Iohanne filio Ormin . uice
comite de Rokesburgo apud Rokesburgum

p.cl: William, king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have allowed, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and Saint Cuthbert, and to his monks at Durham, the donation of Cospatric the earl and his successors, that is to say, Nisbet and the toun of Edrom, and the church in the same toun, with all its chapels and pertinents, in free and perpetual alms, to be held as freely and quitly and honourably as the charters of Earl Cospatric and his successors bear witness and affirm.

As witnesses: Richard, bishop of St Andrews; Nicholas the chancellor; Richard the chaplain; Richard de Morville, the constable; William son of Alan, steward; David Oliphant; Dolphin, the chaplain; John son of Orm, sheriff of Roxburgh.
